summary.intent: Stabilize the system prompt for local LLM KV cache reuse by eliminating per-second datetime changes and volatile node telemetry from the prompt prefix.
summary.outcome: System prompt is now fully stable between turns: datetime moved to a trailing system message injected after conversation history, volatile node telemetry (cpu/mem/disk) removed from the prompt since the existing nodes_list tool already serves it on-demand.

repo: The existing nodes_list tool already returns full telemetry (cpu_usage, mem_available, disk_available), so volatile fields in the system prompt were redundant.
run_agent_loop_streaming is called from many places, so adding a new parameter would be invasive — injecting the datetime message into the history slice in the chat handler was cleaner.
apply_voice_reply_suffix previously needed runtime_context to reposition the datetime tail; removing datetime from the system prompt simplified this to a no-arg call.
serialize_messages_for_request() in openai.rs iterates all messages without filtering by position, so trailing system messages are preserved correctly for all OpenAI-compatible providers.

code.finding: format_node_runtime_line() previously included cpu_usage, mem_available, disk_available, telemetry_stale — all volatile fields now removed, only stable fields remain.
New public runtime_datetime_message() function returns datetime string for injection as a separate trailing ChatMessage::system, replacing append_runtime_datetime_tail().
Datetime system message is injected after conversation history and before the user message in both streaming and non-streaming chat handler paths.
to_openai_value() serializes system messages to {role: system, content: ...} regardless of position in the message list.

workflow: When a function has many call sites, prefer injecting new behavior at a higher level (e.g., the chat handler) rather than modifying the function signature.
The beads issue tracker had database initialization problems in worktrees — fell back to tracking work through commits instead.
Tailwind CSS build is a required step for compilation; a missing CSS file blocks cargo build even for backend-only changes.

friction: Beads database failed to initialize in the worktree, blocking issue creation — had to skip and track work through commits.
Tailwind CSS build was required before cargo compilation would succeed, adding an unexpected build dependency for backend-only changes.
The runtime_context parameter double-wrapping (Option<&T> passed as .as_ref()) caused a compilation error that needed a fix after the initial implementation.
open_items: Phase 4 (Ollama native API support) explicitly parked as future work — only needed if prompt caching via message injection proves insufficient.
Manual validation with a running Ollama + Qwen instance to verify prompt eval time stays small across turns (per the original plan's Validation section) — not yet done.

The plan's original Phase 1 (coarsen datetime to date-only for local providers) was superseded by the end-of-messages approach but could still be a fallback if some local models handle trailing system messages poorly.
